title: 2.0 TC4x - CPU - Use Case 2: Shared memory using hardware protection

content.text: Tricore CPUx with lockstep (LS) monitoring enabled is accessing any of the dedicated memory resources of another TriCor CPUy with LS monitoring enabled, with the system address map in linear mode, SOTA A or SOTA B. Additionally, the CPUx can access the shared memory LMU. In this use case all access are protected by hardware safety mechanisms.
The remote memory resources considered in this Use case are:

items: PSPR, DSPR and DLMU of CPUy
The dedicated PFLASH or PRRAM bank(s) of CPUy
One or more instances of the shared memory LMU

rows: SM[HW]:LMU:ERROR_CORRECTION SM[HW]:LMU:ECC_MONITOR SM[HW]:LMU:ADDRESS SM[HW]:LMU:MBIST SM[HW]:LMU:CONTROL SM[HW]:LMU:SM_CONTROL SM[HW]:LMU:RAM_REG_MONITOR
Same as DSPR refer to 1.0 TC4x - CPU - Use Case 1 -Dedicated memory using system address map - XC-CT China - Docupedia
SM[HW]:LMU:CONTROL_REDUNDANCY

The LMU control redundancy replicates the logic used to control the transfer of data between the [SRI] slave interface and the SSH wrapper around the SRAM. The control redundancy detects permanent and transient faults in the control logic by the way of hardware redundancy, using two independent control logic instances called the master and the checker
This safety mechanism is active after application reset
NA
If this safety mechanism detects a fault, the CPU generates a CPU TriCore lockstep mismatch alarm
SM[HW]:LMU:CONTROL_REDUNDANCY_SCC

A continuously running hardware-based test injects a fault at every input of the redundancy comparator in a sequential manner. A special unit detects that the fault has been propagated to the correct comparator node. If such is not the case either the comparator has a defect or a real fault took place and a lockstep alarm is generated
This safety mechanism is active after application reset.
NA
If this safety mechanism detects a fault, the LMU generates a LMU control redundancy self-test mismatch alarm.
SM[HW]:LMU:READ_WRITE_ECC

The data paths from the SRI interface to the SFRs and SRAM are protected by EDC codes. In addition, the register used to hold data for the last beat in non-aligned burst read access (BTR4, BTR8) is also protected by EDC codes. The EDC codes are checked at the destination and violations are reported
The safety mechanism is active after application reset
NA
On detection of an EDC violation the LMU signals an LMU internal data transfer EDC error alarm to the SMU
SM[HW]:LMU:TRANSACTION_INTEGRITY
The LMU SRI slave implements its role in SM[HW]:SRI:SRI_TRANSACTION_INTEGRITY with different reporting depending on the phase in which an SRI EDC check failure occurs
The safety mechanism is active after application reset
NA
On detection of an EDC violation in the address phase of an access the LMU sets the MEMCON.ADDERR bit and signals an LMU SRI Address Phase EDC Error alarm to the SMU
